name: Swatch / finish variant switching
logic: When a product comes in finishes — kraft vs white vs full-colour print — a text list ('available in 6 finishes') makes the buyer imagine; a row of swatches that re-skins the preview lets them SEE. It's the cheapest upsell you have: showing the premium printed finish beside plain kraft is what moves a buyer up a tier. Baymard found 54% of sites fail to update the product image to the selected variant — buyers should never have to guess what they're actually ordering.

evidence.title: Product Thumbnails Should Update to Match the Variation (54% Don't)
evidence.note: Verified 2026-06-11: '54% to be precise' of e-commerce sites fail to switch the image to the selected colour/variation; dynamic switching is 'absolutely vital'.

evidence.title: Synchronize Data Across Product Variations (28% Don't)
evidence.note: Variation content must stay in sync so buyers don't miss information on the specific option they pick.

exemplars.name: IKEA variant swatches
exemplars.why: Colour/finish swatches re-render the product image on selection — no imagination required.

metricsNote: 54% of sites don't reflect the chosen variant in the image (Baymard, verified).
